goal: Add the first bounded SPG-001 PromptContextReceipt/v1 contract surface without copying Codex prompts or claiming runtime emission.

forbiddenFiles: **/*prompt*/** when used as raw prompt source content rather than a source-ref/hash fixture
**/*transcript*/** when used as raw transcript content rather than a source-ref/hash fixture

nonGoals: Do not collect or persist full Codex system prompts, developer prompts, user prompts, transcripts, or secrets.
Do not claim prompt-context-receipt/v1 is emitted by runtime paths in this slice.
Do not use prompt context as claim-support evidence for closeout, CI, review, Linear, merge readiness, or goal completion.
Do not create a competing prompt composer, tool registry, scheduler, or Codex app-server surface.
implementationApproach: Define a compact TypeScript contract for prompt-context-receipt/v1 with source refs, source hashes, authority layer names, permission posture, goal context refs, selected capability surfaces, redaction status, freshness, and a hard orientation/audit-only evidence-use discriminator.
Publish a JSON Schema and example that validate through the existing runtime packet schema manifest.
Mark runtimeStatus as not_yet_emitted until a later producer slice can bind actual runtime contributors.
Add focused validator regression coverage so the checked-in manifest count and parity validator recognize the new packet.
Add a negative validation fixture or test that rejects embedded raw prompt/transcript-like fields or claim-support evidence use.
riskControls: Source refs must be compact artifact pointers plus optional hashes, not raw prompt text.
The packet must require evidenceUse to be orientation or audit_trail only; claim_support must be rejected by schema or contract validation.
Redaction status is required so unknown or incomplete redaction cannot masquerade as safe runtime proof.
Runtime status remains not_yet_emitted to prevent false-success claims.

reviewStatus.note: Adversarial and best-practices intent findings were adopted before implementation. Agent-native artifact path correction remains required before a final slice done claim.

rollback: Restore runtime packet validator packet count expectations.
